figures.caption: Figure 4: Success rates on collision-rich set with different optimal expansion strategies.
Figure 2: The incremental search tree of CT-TCBS for an instance with 3 agents and 2 tasks. Colours indicate node state: blue for standard, orange for partially assigned, and red for collision.

Figure 5: Solution quality (optimality gap, %) ofWT vs. BT on Random and Spatially-biased scenario sets under sparse-10 maps. WT achieves substantially lower means and variances than BT in both sets, indicating better solution quality.
Figure 6: Success rates of suboptimal task selectors (BT vs. WT) on random (left) and spatially-biased (right) scenarios. Lower is better.
Table 3: Comparison of runtime and solution gap (mean± std) along with their average ranks across 372 common instances. Lower is better. CT-CBS-WT is abbreviated as WT.
